svg.text: excubitor — crash test
excubitor — crash test (60s, zero-install; deletes nothing outside a temp dir)
An unattended loop has decided it is done and reaches for a cleanup:
$ git clean -fd
In its working tree sits an untracked file it never committed:
/tmp/excubitor-demo/UNSAVED_WORK.txt ← no commit, no reflog, no undo
① With the loop guard armed (CLAUDE_LOOP_GUARD=1)
DENIED — the tool call never runs. The guard says:
❯ Blocked: an autonomous loop (CLAUDE_LOOP_GUARD set) may not delete
untracked files (git clean). Loops are stop-and-surface, never
stop-and-act: keep working and committing on your own branch, then
STOP and surface the branch for an out-of-loop reviewer (e.g.
pre-merge-review) or a human to merge. A self-paced loop cannot
bless its own completion — telos discharge is
surface-not-correctness and a loop that writes its own witnesses
routes around the SUSPECT guard. To allow autonomous integration of
verifiable work, set CLAUDE_LOOP_GUARD=yolo instead
(reversible/internal acts only); to lift the guard entirely, unset
it (accepting unattended irreversible VC actions).
② The same act with the guard off (CLAUDE_LOOP_GUARD unset)
guard defers → the runtime runs the command:
$ git clean -fd
Removing UNSAVED_WORK.txt
✗ UNSAVED_WORK.txt is gone. No reflog, no undo. Three hours, unattended.
That is the whole idea. A loop cannot bless its own "done" and then act on it:
the irreversible tail is fenced outside the model, in a PreToolUse hook.
Install ...... scripts/install.sh (symlinks the guards into ~/.claude)
How it decides hooks/guard-loop-vc.py · the full model: THREAT-MODEL.md
Honest limits KNOWN-BYPASSES.md (this is a seatbelt, not a sandbox)
